sessions.lever: consolidation sweep: 38 additional variant forms across pointer-arithmetic spellings, named-intermediate placements, mutation styles, structural rearrangements
sessions.summary: Read v11's i.lreg + i.greg explicitly: pseudo 74 (addu output, = p's value) is the addu's set-target in RTL `(set (reg 74) (plus (reg 79) (reg 78)))` with operand order lw-first sll-second. Pre-allocation: 79->v0 (lw dest), 78->v1 (sll dest), 74->v0. The plus's commutative operand order is what locks pseudo 74 to $v0 (it takes the first source's register family). Pre-RTL, that order is set by the C expression `D_8009BD6C + v*0x10` (load-then-shift). All structural variants that keep the load-then-shift expression order plateau at 2; variants that flip to shift-then-load order (v40/v45/v50/v51/v64/v65) regress to 5-12 because they push the addu out of the if-block or collapse the trampoline. No pure-C surface reverses the plus operand order while preserving the named-intermediate's pseudo 73->[3,4] preference that delivered floor 7->2.

sessions.lever: random permuter (7188 iter, base score 20) + 1 additional structural variant (typed-ptr subscript `p[v*8]` w/ outer pointer assignment)
sessions.summary: Set up directed permuter workspace at permuter/func_80078B04/ (base.c=candidate; target.s from asm/funcs/func_80078B04.s + permuter prelude). 4-thread random permuter ran 7188 iterations, never broke base score 20 (weighted score for the 2-insn diff: addu reg encoding + lhu base). Confirmed: also tried typed-pointer subscript form `u16 *p = (u16 *)D_8009BD6C; result = p[v*8];` — regressed to score 7 (same regression family as shift-first variants). Random permuter + 74 manual variants + new structural variant: random-mutation surface is exhausted. Floor 2 plateau is real. The remaining 2-insn diff requires either (a) a directed-PERM permuter probe with explicit axis macros that random-only can't hit, OR (b) a non-randomization-discoverable C-shape that flips RTL `(plus a b)` operand order without disturbing the named-intermediate's allocno preference.

prior_sessions_summary: Session 1 (2026-06-12, head fe15e792): u16 *p named intermediate (split addr expr from deref). Floor 7->2. Reviewer PASS.

reviewer.evidence: u16 *p is a live named intermediate (assigned + dereferenced); analogous to SOTN-sanctioned named-intermediate-declaration-order family (no-new-park-categories.md SOTN-accepted). Not a forbidden cheat family; passes all 6 checklist tests. Candidate unchanged across sessions 1, 2, 3 — verdict carries forward.

remaining_gap.summary: Coupled allocator tiebreak. Insn 22 in i.greg: `(set 74 (plus 79 78))` where 79=lw(D_8009BD6C)->$v0, 78=sll(v,4)->$v1. Output 74 takes $v0 because (a) it's the plus's first source's register and (b) downstream (lhu base, then result return) all want $v0. Target needs `(set 78 (plus 78 79))` so 78 (now also addu dest) stays in $v1, freeing the lhu's dest from base==dest collapse. Reversing the plus order requires reversing the C expression order, but every shift-first form (v40 ofs+D_..., v45 ofs+=, v50 sv+=, v64 m2c-form, v65 m2c-form-p, typed-ptr-subscript) breaks either the named-intermediate's preference push OR the trampoline-shape (collapses to 11-13 build_insns). Random permuter 7188 iter confirms no random C-shape closes this on the candidate's structural surface.
remaining_gap.rules_required_for_full_match: HEAD has $2 <-> $3 @3-6 + subst $4->$3 @0,1,3 + lhu $2,0($2)->$2,0($3) @7 — 5 rules. Candidate would still need 2 substs: addu output rename (74->v1) + lhu base rewrite.

next_hypotheses: Directed permuter with explicit PERM_* macros. Random permuter is exhausted (7188 iter at score 20 floor). Try PERM_GENERAL on the address-expression site (`D_8009BD6C + v * 0x10`) + PERM_TEMP_FOR_EXPR on the dereferenced pointer + PERM_INT_TYPE on `v`. Workspace already set up at permuter/func_80078B04/. Rewrite base.c to: `result = PERM_GENERAL(*PERM_TEMP_FOR_EXPR((u16 *)(D_8009BD6C + v * 0x10)));` and re-run with longer budget (~30k iters minimum) — directed mode explores axes random can't.

GCC source dive — `tools/gcc-2.7.2/expr.c` (`expand_expr`, `simplify_binary_operation`) and `combine.c` (`simplify_logical`, `simplify_comparison`). Locate the canonicalization rule for commutative PLUS during RTL expansion. If pseudo number ordering or operand 'simpler' classification controls the swap, identify a C-shape that creates the sll-feeding pseudo at a lower number than the lw-feeding pseudo. Specifically read mips.c's `mips_legitimize_address` for any plus-canonicalization on the target side.

Sibling cross-ref via instrumented dumps — `BB2_ALLOC_DEBUG=1 BB2_PRIO_DEBUG=1 tmp/gccdbg/cc1` on BOTH the candidate and a synthesized standalone of func_80078A68's body (sibling that matched with `(t0 * 0x10) + D_8009BD6C` shift-first), diff the allocno-preference and conflict-graph dumps at the addu insn. A68 is a STORE-base-reuse pattern, but its addu lands sll-first naturally — what shape of the input C drove that?

rejected_forms.reason: Improves direction (in-place addu) but mask still lives in $a0 because v is the accumulator and GCC keeps the original arg's register. Not a cheat — just doesn't reach 2.
Hoists the pointer load OUT of the if-true branch — target's lw is INSIDE the if. Structural mismatch raises distance.

rejected_forms.form: register T x asm("$3") pin or single-instruction __asm__ to force $v1
rejected_forms.reason: register-asm pin + hardcoded-$N asm injection — forbidden by inline-asm-policy.md and inline-asm-injection.md.

rejected_forms.form: shift-first expression order family (v40 ofs+D_..., v45 ofs+=, v50 sv+=, v46 ofs+D_cast, v64/v65 m2c early-return forms, p[v*8] typed-ptr subscript w/ outer base assign)
rejected_forms.reason: Reversing the C operand to put the shift on the LEFT regresses the score. Either kills the named-intermediate preference (back to score 7) or breaks the if/else trampoline structure (collapses build_insns to 11-13, score 8-12).

rejected_forms.form: named-intermediate variants holding floor 2 (v37 ofs+p, v39 byteptr, v41 addr_arith, v42 sv_intermediate, v43 pluseq_byte, v44 struct_alias, v47 base_idx, v48 base_idx_inline, v49 sv_then_p, v52 u32 addr cast, v54 v_extends, v55 struct.w arrow, v57-60 various, v68 uint_p, v69-74 various)
rejected_forms.reason: All preserve the candidate's named-intermediate allocno-preference push (mask->$v1) but cannot reverse the plus's RTL operand order. Confirms the floor-2 plateau is a real coupled tiebreak, not a lever-search shallowness issue. ~38 variants across this session, ~36 in prior session = ~74 explored forms.
Mild regression vs s32 v — the unsigned mask interaction with slt nudges the comparison RTL. No closing path.

rejected_forms.form: Random permuter (7188 iter, base score 20, --stop-on-zero, 4 threads)
rejected_forms.reason: Random-mutation surface around the candidate is exhausted. No iteration broke base score; many regressed to 100s-1500s. Closing form, if it exists, requires directed PERM macros OR a structural lever outside permuter's randomization repertoire.
